title: Anchorage Daily News: Alaska's Newspaper
summary: Use a little angling imagination FISHING REPORT: Define your passions, research the possibilities. By KEN MARSH Anchorage Daily News Published: September 2, 2004 Last Modified: May 30, 2005 at 04:40 AM The fishing Tuesday was exquisite -- the number of fish, their relative size and the variety of species caught was comparable to what you'd expect from a remote Southwest Alaska river. There were rainbow trout, Dolly Varden and some of the biggest grayling I've seen this side of Ugashik Narrows. All lurked among the scarlet forms of pre-spawn king salmon, which the smaller fish followed much as wolves shadow migrating caribou herds. I spent the day working my way upriver, wading and casting where I could, sometimes busting through boreal jungle to reach the next gravel bar. Each pool held it…

title: Anchorage Daily News: Alaska's Newspaper
summary: State biologists ask hunters to leave pack goats at home NOT A LAW: Animals can transmit highly contagious diseases to Dall sheep. By DAN JOLING The Associated Press Published: August 10th, 2005 Last Modified: August 10th, 2005 at 01:36 AM Dall sheep hunters planning to use domestic goats as pack animals have been asked by state veterinarians to leave them at home. Spokesmen for the state Department of Environmental Conservation and the Department of Fish and Game said Tuesday that domestic goats or llamas could expose wild Dall sheep, mountain goats or musk oxen to diseases or parasites. Dall sheep hunting begins today in Interior Alaska. Dr. Kimberlee Beckmen, a Fish and Game Department wildlife veterinarian in Fairbanks, said using goats as pack animals was the talk of Fairbanks on Tues…
